snopes.title: Did a German hotel send 'no Jews allowed' message to Israeli guest?
snopes.description: The hotel said the antisemitic response was sent after it mistook the Israeli booking inquiry for a fake request.

snopes.title: Did murderer named Ronald McDonald kill 12 children in 1892? Here's the truth
snopes.description: According to the story, a late-19th-century serial killer shared a name with the future fast-food mascot.

snopes.title: Did Netanyahu once say, 'America is a golden calf and we will suck it dry'? There's no proof
snopes.description: The alleged quote from Israel's prime minister has repeatedly resurfaced online since 2015, but evidence remains elusive.

snopes.title: Was Florida woman arrested for handing out tequila samples in Costco?
snopes.description: The story originated from social media posts by The Dude Humor Report, which labels its content as satirical.

snopes.title: Did slaves who escaped to Canada invent ice hockey? Unpacking the real history
snopes.description: Black Canadians didn't "invent" hockey, but some of the playstyles commonplace today originated from Black pioneers in the sport.

snopes.title: Is Coleman lazy river pool real and sold at Costco?
snopes.description: Social media users shared an image promoting a "200-foot lazy river pool system," complete with inner tubes, a filter pump and a chlorine dispenser.

snopes.title: Trump dozed off in Oval Office. Did he wake himself up with a fart, as video allegedly shows?
snopes.description: Social media clips purportedly showed the president waking himself up with a fart after dozing off during an Oval Office event.

snopes.title: Did residents of French village ask Hegseth to skip D-Day ceremony?
snopes.description: It's unclear why the defense secretary didn't attend the ceremony in Langrune-sur-Mer.

snopes.title: Unfounded claim Melania Trump was an escort before meeting her husband recirculates online
snopes.description: Melania Trump has repeatedly denied the rumor since it first appeared in 2016, even winning a lawsuit against the Daily Mail.

snopes.title: Are US, Israeli militaries integrating? Here's what US defense bill says
snopes.description: Social media users falsely claimed the two militaries would fully merge. While the bill outlines closer collaboration, many details remain unclear.

snopes.title: Was Florida woman arrested after riding shopping cart down highway? Not so fast
snopes.description: According to the tale, the woman had fashioned a sail out of a shower curtain.

snopes.title: Did police arrest woman for posing as Costco employee, handing out meatball samples?
snopes.description: According to the story, police took Sienna Halverson, 31, into custody in Glendale, Arizona.

snopes.title: Step away from claim 'House Bill 417' banned line dancing in several US states
snopes.description: The claim is part of a trend of social media posts, often produced using AI tools, sharing fake legislation.

snopes.title: Chick-fil-A didn't post statement about refusing to celebrate Pride month
snopes.description: The fast food chain has had a checkered history with regard to LGBTQ+ rights.

snopes.title: Mark Twain falsely credited with quote about reading 'some obituaries with great pleasure'
snopes.description: Social media memes and items for purchase, including magnets, stickers and yard signs, have misattributed the quote to the American author.

snopes.title: 20 claims we've fact-checked about Bill Clinton
snopes.description: From AI-generated criticisms of Trump officials to alleged hush money, here are some of our most popular investigations regarding the ex-president.

snopes.title: Image of girl washing Trump and Epstein's feet is fake. Here's how we know
snopes.description: The image was created by Alison Jackson, an artist who makes "convincingly realistic" images.

snopes.title: Will Utah data center use 16B gallons of water and span almost 3 Manhattans? What to know
snopes.description: The center, backed by Canadian billionaire Kevin O'Leary, has faced relentless pushback from communities near the proposed site.

snopes.title: Pervasive 'perfume shock' warning is decades-old hoax
snopes.description: Social media users claimed thieves used drugged perfume to knock women out and rob them.

snopes.title: 25 claims about Pride Month we've inspected
snopes.description: Whether you're deep in the weeds of Pride discourse or just now learning what a pronoun is, Snopes is here to give you the facts.

factcheck.title: Ohio Senate Candidates Spar Over Donations Tied, Loosely or Not, to Epstein
factcheck.description: Ohio voters are witnessing a battle of campaign television ads as each Senate candidate tries to tie the other to Jeffrey Epstein -- by way of donations from those with some link to the late convicted sex offender. The post Ohio Senate Candidates Spar Over Donations Tied, Loosely or Not, to Epstein appeared first on FactCheck.org .

factcheck.title: Trump's Push to Make Daylight Saving Time Permanent
factcheck.description: Legislative efforts to make daylight saving time permanent year round got a boost with support from President Donald Trump, who criticized the twice-yearly clock switching as cost-prohibitive. But there is no strong evidence that Trump's solution -- switching permanently to daylight saving time -- would provide the economic boost Trump suggests it would. The post Trump's Push to Make Daylight Saving Time Permanent appeared first on FactCheck.org .

factcheck.title: Trump Misrepresents Climate Change Scenarios
factcheck.description: In a May 16 Truth Social post, President Donald Trump cited updated climate change scenarios to misleadingly claim that experts had “admitted” prior climate change projections “were WRONG! WRONG! WRONG!” The regularly scheduled revision reflects in part the progress the world has made on moving away from fossil fuels. The post Trump Misrepresents Climate Change Scenarios appeared first on FactCheck.org .

factcheck.title: Stopping Fraud Won't 'Save' Social Security, Create 'Balanced Budget,' as Trump Suggests
factcheck.description: The Trump administration says it's working to reduce the amount of fraud in federal government programs. However, fiscal experts have said that those reductions alone won't "save" the Social Security program or result in "a balanced budget," as President Donald Trump has falsely suggested. The post Stopping Fraud Won't 'Save' Social Security, Create 'Balanced Budget,' as Trump Suggests appeared first on FactCheck.org .

factcheck.title: A Timeline of RFK Jr.'s Mixed Messaging on the Measles Vaccine
factcheck.description: In defending his record on measles, Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. twice said during a recent Senate hearing, “We promote" the measles vaccine. While it’s true that the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention continues to recommend the shot, Kennedy has rarely made an unequivocal endorsement of it, even as the nation has seen an alarming rise in measles cases. The post A Timeline of RFK Jr.'s Mixed Messaging on the Measles Vaccine appeared first on FactCheck.org .

factcheck.title: Trump Distorts Maryland's Primary Ballot Mix-up to Attack Mail-in Voting
factcheck.description: Maryland election officials are mailing replacement ballots to voters after a vendor error led to some voters receiving a mail-in ballot for the wrong political party's primary in June. However, in criticizing the mix-up, President Donald Trump distorted the facts to claim that 500,000 “fake,” "corrupt" and "illegal" ballots had been mailed to ensure “Democrats win.” The post Trump Distorts Maryland's Primary Ballot Mix-up to Attack Mail-in Voting appeared first on FactCheck.org .

factcheck.title: RFK Jr.’s Muddled Claims on Drug Approval Speed
factcheck.description: The Food and Drug Administration in recent months has approved a small number of drugs quite quickly under a new expedited review program. But Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has overstated the impact of the program by making misleading comparisons to the pace of drug approvals in the past. The post RFK Jr.’s Muddled Claims on Drug Approval Speed appeared first on FactCheck.org .

factcheck.title: Ad in Texas GOP Runoff Attacks Cornyn on Immigration, Islam
factcheck.description: A TV ad attacking incumbent Texas Sen. John Cornyn for supporting "Muslim mass immigration" misleadingly cites a 2021 quote from Cornyn about certain Afghan refugees and claims that "Cornyn has a special place in his heart for radical Islam." The post Ad in Texas GOP Runoff Attacks Cornyn on Immigration, Islam appeared first on FactCheck.org .

factcheck.title: What Will Happen to Gasoline Prices When the Iran War Ends?
factcheck.description: President Donald Trump on multiple occasions has assured the public that high gasoline prices will “rapidly” or “quickly” decline “as soon as” the war with Iran ends. Energy experts told us that prices will start to fall when the conflict is resolved, but it could take many months before the national average price is back to where it was before the conflict began. The post What Will Happen to Gasoline Prices When the Iran War Ends? appeared first on FactCheck.org .

factcheck.title: Who's Paying for the White House Ballroom?
factcheck.description: Q: Has President Trump asked for a billion dollars for the ballroom? A: Since the White House announced plans in July for a ballroom, the president has promised to fund its construction without using public money. But in May congressional Republicans proposed $1 billion in federal funding for "security adjustments and upgrades" including at the White House and the ballroom site. FULL ANSWER President Donald Trump has claimed that the new White House ballroom would be privately funded, The post Who's Paying for the White House Ballroom? appeared first on FactCheck.org .
